title: SQLi WAF Bypass Techniques
when_to_use: When basic SQLi payloads are blocked

steps.title: Initial Setup/Scanning
steps.instruction: Attempt SQL injection with case variation (mixed uppercase/lowercase keywords)
steps.tip: Start with the most reliable information source for sqli waf bypass techniques
steps.expected_output: WAF bypass confirmation if mixed-case payloads bypass filters

steps.title: Data Collection/Configuration
steps.instruction: Test comment injection techniques using SQL comment syntax to obfuscate queries
steps.tip: Organize data systematically to avoid duplicates and ensure coverage
steps.expected_output: Confirmed bypass if comments are effective at evading pattern matching

steps.title: Analysis/Enumeration
steps.instruction: Apply URL encoding to payload characters to obscure malicious strings
steps.tip: Look for patterns and correlations in collected data
steps.expected_output: Double-encoded payload successfully bypassing simple string filters

steps.title: Advanced Technique/Filtering
steps.instruction: Test double encoding where already encoded strings are encoded again
steps.tip: Use multiple techniques to increase coverage and accuracy
steps.expected_output: Confirmation that double-encoded payloads bypass multi-layer filtering

steps.title: Correlation/Testing
steps.instruction: Utilize inline MySQL comments (/*! MySQL-specific */) to obfuscate SQL keywords
steps.tip: Validate findings across different tools and methods
steps.expected_output: Working SQLi payload using inline comment syntax specific to database type

steps.title: Validation/Exploitation
steps.instruction: Use sqlmap with tamper scripts like space2comment to automate WAF evasion
steps.expected_output: Successful SQLi exploitation using automated tamper script obfuscation

steps.title: Documentation/Reporting
steps.instruction: Test and document each successful bypass technique with screenshots
steps.tip: Review all findings before compilation for accuracy and completeness
steps.expected_output: Documented report of all working WAF bypass techniques with proof-of-concept payloads
